era: Hip-Hop. E=5 -- mainstream breakthrough, production partnership, independent chart success. Macklemore & Ryan Lewis. Two #1 Billboard hits, independently released. Four Grammys. Spokane enters as second non-Seattle city. Twenty-seventh degree in hip-hop era (134-160). v1.49.300.

location.formative: Spokane -- eastern Washington, 280 miles from Seattle. Different climate, economy, culture. Spokane-to-Seattle migration for music career.
location.career: Ryan Lewis. Producer half of Macklemore & Ryan Lewis. Horn-driven production. Independent mainstream breakthrough. 2006-present.

members.name: Ryan Lewis
members.note: Spokane origin. Horn-driven, anthemic production. Photography/video background. The essential but often unseen partner.

associated_musicians.name: Macklemore (Ben Haggerty)
associated_musicians.note: Capitol Hill, Seattle. The lyrical and public face of the duo. Lewis provides the sonic foundation.

associated_musicians.name: Ray Dalton
associated_musicians.note: Featured vocalist on 'Can't Hold Us.' The vocal hook.

associated_musicians.name: Mary Lambert
associated_musicians.note: Featured vocalist on 'Same Love.' Marriage equality anthem.

theory_nodes.concept: Independent mainstream proof -- #1 Billboard without major label. First since 1994. Paradigm-shifting demonstration. Self-fund, self-release, internet + touring = competitive at the highest level. Industry impact.
examples: Can't Hold Us: #1 Billboard Hot 100. Independent.
First independently distributed #1 since Lisa Loeb (1994).
Four Grammy Awards (2014). Best New Artist. Best Rap Album.
theory_nodes.description: Independent #1. Paradigm shift. Self-released at the top.

genealogy.relationship: Macklemore & Ryan Lewis prove the model at 160.

theory_nodes.concept: Spokane enters -- second non-Seattle city of origin. Eastern Washington. 280 miles from Seattle. Different climate, economy, culture. Spokane-to-Seattle migration pattern. Geographic reach of the engine expands.
examples: Tacoma (143): first non-Seattle city. South Sound.
Spokane (160): second non-Seattle city. Eastern Washington.
Lewis migrated Spokane to Seattle for music career.
theory_nodes.description: Spokane enters. Second non-Seattle. Geographic expansion.

genealogy.relationship: Ryan Lewis at 160. Spokane enters the engine.

theory_nodes.concept: Horn-driven anthemic production -- prominent brass sections, arena-sized hooks, clean punchy drums, radio-ready mix. Lewis's signature: accessibility with depth. The sound that reached #1.
examples: Can't Hold Us: horn riff = one of most recognizable in 2010s pop.
Thrift Shop: saxophone-driven hook. Swagger tempo.
Same Love: piano + strings. Emotional production.

cross_references.relationship: PRODUCER MODELS! Sango: producer-as-artist, baile funk, self-released. Lewis: producer-in-partnership, horn-driven, independent mainstream. Different approaches to the producer role.
GEOGRAPHIC EXPANSION! First non-Seattle (Tacoma, 143). Second non-Seattle (Spokane, 160). 17 degrees apart. The engine's geographic reach.
SUCCESS SPECTRUM! Shabazz Palaces: experimental success (Sub Pop, critical acclaim). Ryan Lewis: mainstream success (#1 Billboard, Grammys). Both valid. Different scales.
PRODUCER EVOLUTION! Sabzi's boom-bap (99) to Sango's baile funk (159) to Lewis's horn-driven anthem (160). Three production paradigms across 61 degrees.
